item.title: Uber Tightens US Driver Background Checks as Sexual Assault Cases Mount
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is tightening driver background checks in the US and applying the new standards retroactively to existing workers, the company’s latest response to a wave of sexual-assault lawsuits brought by passengers.

item.title: Exxon’s Top US Gas Trader to Join Expand as Exits Hit Oil Giant
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp.’s head of US gas and power trading is moving to Expand Energy Corp., one of several recent departures from a unit the supermajor has been trying to grow.

item.title: What Is AI Distillation and Why Is It a Worry for the Industry?
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies in the US have spent hundreds of billions of dollars to develop more advanced chatbots, betting they can earn enough from customers to justify the investment. That approach carries the risk of being undermined by rivals who build competing AI systems for far less.

item.title: Stock Valuations Should Worry Investors, Abby Joseph Cohen Says
item.description: Lofty stock prices may be hiding risks investors should be paying more attention to, especially in the labor market, according to Abby Joseph Cohen, the former Goldman Sachs partner and now Columbia Business School professor.

item.title: Venezuela Quakes Did $6.7 Billion in Direct Damage, UN Estimates
item.description: Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes this week affected nearly two million buildings, causing $6.7 billion in direct damage, according to estimates by a United Nations body.

item.title: China Ousts Six Military Lawmakers as Xi’s Purge Continues
item.description: China stripped six military lawmakers of their seats in the national parliament in the latest sign that President Xi Jinping is continuing with a purge of key defense establishment personnel.

item.title: OpenAI Limits Release of New Model Under Pressure From US
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a preview version of a more capable new artificial intelligence model to select partners before making it available more widely in the coming weeks, following pressure from the Trump administration to stagger the release.

item.title: Trump Vows 100% Tariff on Countries Over Digital Services Taxes
item.description: President Donald Trump threatened to impose 100% tariffs on goods imported from countries that impose digital services taxes, escalating pressure on European nations that just ratified a trade pact with the US.

item.title: Brookfield Is in Fight Over Solar Firm Where Cash ‘Evaporated’
item.description: Joh. Berenberg, Gossler & Co. KG and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are battling over the future of GoldenPeaks, an energy company staring at collapse after borrowing $1.5 billion for European solar-power projects.

item.title: BlackBerry CEO Says Safety Software Offers AI-Resistant Growth
item.description: BlackBerry Ltd. Chief Executive Officer John Giamatteo had a direct answer when asked about one of the biggest challenges to software providers: Artificial intelligence isn’t ready to displace his safety-certified products.

item.title: Crypto’s ETF Boom Gets $4.5 Billion Reality Check in Brutal Week
item.description: The investors who were supposed to bring stability to Bitcoin are heading for the exits.

item.title: Oil Tanker Earnings Plunge by $200,000 as More Return to Hormuz
item.description: Oil tanker earnings tumbled as more ships are willing to enter the Strait of Hormuz, capping a dramatic week of swings in costs to hire the world’s biggest crude carriers.

item.title: Colombia’s ‘Tiger Trade’ Fades as Fiscal Risks Loom
item.description: The rally that accompanied Abelardo de la Espriella’s rise to power in Colombia is already fading, with investors waiting on cabinet announcements and a concrete plan to repair the nation’s strained public finances before adding to what had been some of the best trades in emerging markets.

item.title: Morgan Stanley-Led Group to Sell Chicago Meters for $2.5 Billion
item.description: New York-based Stonepeak Partners will pay $2.5 billion to buy the remainder of a lease of Chicago’s parking meters, according to a spokesperson for the firm.

item.title: There’s Been a Subtle Shift in the AI Zeitgeist
item.description: Investor enthusiasm is rebalancing.

item.title: Canadian Testing Company CSA Taps Jefferies to Explore a Sale
item.description: CSA Group, the Canadian product testing and certification company, is exploring a sale that could value the business at around C$2 billion ($1.4 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: SpaceX Index Era Sets Up Clash Among Shorts and Passive Billions
item.description: SpaceX’s stock has started to lose momentum after its blockbuster market debut. But investors betting against Elon Musk’s newest public company will soon face a different force: billions of dollars in mechanical buying as the stock enters major indexes.

item.title: Immigrants Go From White-Collar Careers to US Shadow Workforce
item.description: Efforts to dismantle visa programs are upending immigrants' lives and roiling businesses that depend on them.

item.title: California to Vote on Raising Bar for Cities to Increase Taxes
item.description: California voters will decide in November whether to approve a measure that would make it significantly harder to hike local taxes through the ballot box.

item.title: Kashkari Says Fed May Need Rate Hike Amid Broad Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari said signs of widespread inflation led him to pencil in one interest-rate increase for this year in the central bank’s economic projections released earlier this month.

item.title: Euro Zone Probably Saw First Inflation Slowdown Since Iran War Began
item.description: The euro zone’s first signs of respite in inflation since the outbreak of the Iran war are forecast for data next week that will reflect the impact of subsiding energy costs.

item.title: Mercedes, GIC to Back Autonomous Driving Firm Momenta’s IPO
item.description: Momenta Global Ltd., the Chinese autonomous driving startup, has roped in carmaker Mercedes-Benz AG and Singapore wealth fund GIC Pte as cornerstone investors for its Hong Kong IPO, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Morgan Stanley, Goldman Shares Fall on Possible OpenAI IPO Delay
item.description: Shares of Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. fell on Friday after OpenAI is said to weigh holding off on an initial public offering until next year amid volatility in technology stocks.

item.title: Apple’s Touch MacBook to Use M5 Pro and Max Chips, With M7 Models to Follow
item.description: Apple Inc.’s first-ever touch-screen laptop will rely on the company’s current high-end M5 chips, rather than next-generation silicon, though the company is working on a follow-up model with future M7 processors.

item.title: Economists Slash Canada 2026 Growth Outlook After Recession Talk
item.description: A surprise economic slump to start the year prompted forecasters to slash their expectations for Canada’s growth for 2026.

item.title: Brazil’s Top Pork Exporter Boosting Output to Target Asia Demand
item.description: Brazil’s largest pork exporter, cooperative Aurora, is expanding capacity as it bets on rising demand from Asia, underscoring the growing importance of the protein in global meat markets.

item.title: Johannesburg Struggles to Keep Lights on But Repays Bondholders
item.description: The City of Johannesburg, which is on the verge of financial collapse, put investors at the front of its queue of creditors this week when it repaid a 1.44 billion rand ($88 million) bond.

item.title: OpenAI Weighs IPO in 2027 After Expected Anthropic Public Debut
item.description: OpenAI is considering an initial public offering as soon as in 2027, according to people familiar with the matter, a timeline that would potentially see it go public after its artificial intelligence rival Anthropic PBC.

item.title: China Three Gorges Is Said In Talks to Buy German Wind Assets
item.description: China Three Gorges Corp. is in talks to buy a German wind portfolio from NEAG Norddeutsche Energie AG, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
item.description: From data center backlash to boundless cash from tech billionaires and concerns about deepfaked campaign ads, AI is everywhere in the 2026 US elections.
